Why This Tool Exists
Purpose of This Reader

This parallel reader places the canonical LDS text alongside a faithful modern English translation so that the meaning of every verse is immediately accessible to any reader, regardless of their familiarity with scriptural language.

Highlight verses, write annotations and personal notes, and keep a daily journal of your scripture study — all in one place.

📜
Canonical Text
The left panel shows the LDS scriptural text exactly as written.
📖
Modern Translation
The right panel gives a faithful word-for-word rendering in contemporary English.
🖊
Annotations
Highlight verses with color and add personal notes tied to each verse.
📓
Daily Journal
Keep a personal study journal with dated reflections and entries.
About the Text
Introduction to the Book of Mormon
"The Book of Mormon is a volume of holy scripture comparable to the Bible. It is a record of God's dealings with ancient inhabitants of the Americas and contains the fulness of the everlasting gospel."
— Introduction, The Book of Mormon

The Book of Mormon follows multiple narrative threads: the family of Lehi, who fled Jerusalem before its Babylonian conquest and sailed to the Americas; the civilizations their descendants built; the ministry of Jesus Christ among these people following his resurrection; and the final collapse of Nephite civilization, preserved by the prophet Mormon and his son Moroni.
